rag_rules: Repair unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers in AGREE expressions are either visible AADL features or declared as eq/const helpers in the same annex.
Fix type mismatches by aligning expression types with declared variable types using int, real, or bool; avoid implicit conversions.
generic_rules: Only use implementation-level assignments for output ports or auxiliary variables declared in the implementation annex.
Guarantees must reference only directly visible entities or helpers defined in the same annex; never infer subcomponent paths or properties.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use 'eq name : type = expression;' to define helper variables before referencing them in guarantees.
Ensure all numeric comparisons use real or int types; never use Boolean or opaque ports for arithmetic relations.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) or unit tokens as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not attempt to repair type errors by adding architecture—trace and resolve the root unresolved identifier first.
irrelevant_topics: component type vs implementation scope decision
